# fpcloud_bucket_key (Resource)

Manages a scoped S3 access key for a Fogpipe bucket. The secret access key is returned once, on creation.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A scoped, read-only S3 access key for a bucket. The secret access key is only
# returned once, at creation — Terraform keeps it in state (marked sensitive).
resource "fpcloud_bucket_key" "readonly" {
  bucket_id = fpcloud_bucket.assets.id
  name      = "cdn-reader"
  read      = true
  write     = false
  owner     = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bucket_id` (String) ID of the bucket this key is scoped to. Changing it forces a new key.

### Optional

- `name` (String) Optional human-readable name for the key. Changing it forces a new key.
- `owner` (Boolean) Grants owner (bucket-admin) access. Mutable in place.
- `read` (Boolean) Grants read access. Mutable in place.
- `write` (Boolean) Grants write access. Mutable in place.

### Read-Only

- `access_key_id` (String) S3 access key ID.
- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the key was created.
- `id` (String) Access key ID (the key's identity).
- `secret_access_key` (String, Sensitive) S3 secret access key. Returned only on creation — an imported key leaves this empty.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import a bucket key by "bucket_id/access_key_id". The secret access key is not
# recoverable on import (it is returned only at creation).
terraform import fpcloud_bucket_key.readonly bkt-abc123/GK1234567890abcdef
```
